name: SaaS Revenue Glossary
description: Business glossary for SaaS revenue metrics, subscription lifecycle and billing. Aligns with the SaaS Revenue Metrics IPC, the SaaS Revenue Business Event Matrix and the Stripe Customer/Subscription/Invoice data contracts.

terms.name: Customer
terms.description: An organisation or individual that has a paid subscription to one of our SaaS products. Identified end-to-end by a Stripe customer id (`raw.stripe.customers.id`).

relatedConcepts.name: Customer
relatedConcepts.description: Person or organisation paying for a subscription

relatedQuestions.name: How many active paying customers do we have today?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: that has at least one active @{Subscription}

terms.name: Subscription
terms.description: A recurring billing commitment between a Customer and one of our SaaS products. Backed by `raw.stripe.subscriptions`. A customer can have multiple subscriptions (e.g. one per product, plus add-ons).

relatedQuestions.name: How many active subscriptions are billing this month?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: between a @{Customer} and a product, billed on a fixed cadence

terms.name: Invoice
terms.description: A billing document issued against a Subscription. Tracks amount due, amount paid, currency, status, and recognition period. Backed by `raw.stripe.invoices`.

relatedConcepts.name: Invoice
relatedConcepts.description: Billing document for a subscription period

relatedQuestions.name: What is the collected vs outstanding invoice amount this month?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: issued against a @{Subscription} for one billing period

terms.name: Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR)
terms.description: The normalised monthly value of all active subscriptions on a given date. Annual plans are divided by 12; multi-year plans by the contract length. Excludes one-off charges, taxes and refunds.

relatedQuestions.name: What is the Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) trend over the last 12 months?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: summing the normalised monthly value of every active @{Subscription} on a given date

terms.name: Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR)
terms.description: MRR multiplied by 12. Forward-looking expectation of recurring revenue over the next year, assuming no churn or expansion.

relatedQuestions.name: How much Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) are we generating across all customer segments?

terms.name: Average Revenue Per User (ARPU)
terms.description: MRR divided by the number of active paying customers in the period. Tracked per segment to expose where pricing power is weakest.

relatedQuestions.name: What is the Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) for each customer segment?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: equal to @{Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR)} ÷ active @{Customer} count

terms.name: Churn
terms.description: The loss of a Customer or the downgrade/cancellation of a Subscription within a period. Reported as Logo Churn (count of customers lost) and Revenue Churn (MRR lost). Default window: monthly.

relatedConcepts.name: Churn
relatedConcepts.description: Customer or revenue lost in a period

relatedQuestions.name: What is our monthly churn rate by customer segment?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: where a @{Customer} cancels or downgrades a @{Subscription} within a period

terms.name: Expansion Revenue
terms.description: Incremental MRR from existing Customers — upgrades, seat additions, cross-sells of additional products. Counted in the period the expansion event occurs.

relatedConcepts.name: Expansion
relatedConcepts.description: Growth within an existing customer base

relatedQuestions.name: How much of our MRR growth this quarter came from expansion vs new logos?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: where an existing @{Customer} increases their @{Subscription} value

terms.name: Net Revenue Retention (NRR)
terms.description: Percentage of starting MRR retained from existing customers after expansion, contraction and churn. NRR > 100% means the existing customer base is growing on its own.

relatedQuestions.name: What is our Net Revenue Retention (NRR) cohort over time?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: equal to (starting MRR + @{Expansion Revenue} − contraction − @{Churn}) ÷ starting MRR

terms.name: Trial
terms.description: A time-limited free use of a product before billing begins. Tracked on Stripe Subscriptions via `trial_start` / `trial_end`. A trial that doesn't convert to a paid subscription is not counted in MRR.

relatedConcepts.name: Trial
relatedConcepts.description: Free pre-paid period of a subscription

relatedQuestions.name: What percentage of trials convert to paid subscriptions?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: on a @{Subscription} during which the @{Customer} is not yet billed

terms.name: Conversion
terms.description: The lifecycle event where a Trial becomes a paid Subscription. Conversion Rate = converted / trial-started for a cohort. Drives top-of-funnel MRR growth.

relatedQuestions.name: What is the trial-to-paid conversion rate by acquisition channel?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: where a @{Trial} transitions to a billing @{Subscription}

terms.name: Customer Lifetime Value (LTV)
terms.description: Predicted total revenue from a customer over the entire relationship. Simplest formula: ARPU ÷ churn rate. Drives the maximum tolerable Customer Acquisition Cost.

relatedQuestions.name: What is our LTV-to-CAC ratio by segment?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: estimating total revenue from a @{Customer} over the lifetime of the relationship

terms.name: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC)
terms.description: Total sales + marketing spend in a period divided by the number of new paying customers acquired in the same period. Compared against LTV to gauge unit economics.

relatedQuestions.name: What did it cost to acquire a new customer this quarter?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: equal to acquisition spend ÷ new @{Customer} count in a period

terms.name: Recognised Revenue
terms.description: Revenue counted in the period the underlying service was delivered, regardless of when the customer was billed. ASC 606 / IFRS 15. Distinct from cash collected.

relatedQuestions.name: What is our recognised revenue vs collected cash this quarter?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: allocating an @{Invoice} amount to the period the service was delivered

terms.name: Deferred Revenue
terms.description: Cash collected from a Customer for service not yet delivered (e.g. annual upfront payment for monthly service). Sits on the balance sheet as a liability until recognised.

relatedConcepts.name: Deferred Revenue
relatedConcepts.description: Cash received but service not yet delivered

relatedQuestions.name: What is our deferred revenue balance at month-end?

terms.definitionDifferentiator: for cash collected from a @{Customer} but not yet @{Recognised Revenue}
